subject: repo root — missing pyproject.toml
principal: The repo has no `pyproject.toml`. Python files are run via `python -m skills.leash_for_hooks.verify` from the working directory; there's no installable package, no declared dependencies, no version pinning. The repo is not consumable as a Python package.
interest: Anyone who clones the repo and runs `pip install -e .` gets nothing. Dependencies (when added — e.g., Pydantic, radon, prov) cannot be declared. The project is not discoverable by standard Python tooling.
payoff: Add pyproject.toml conforming to PEP 518 (build-system, https://peps.python.org/pep-0518/) and PEP 621 (project metadata, https://peps.python.org/pep-0621/). Declare project name, version, Python version requirement, and dependencies as they accrete. Use setuptools or hatch as the build backend.
re_trigger: any debt requires a non-stdlib dependency (D-001 needs prov-python, D-002 needs pydantic, D-003 needs radon) — paying any of those debts requires paying D-004 first
